mnemonics: UDP: Unreliable, Datagram, Post. Like sending a postcard – it's simple and fast, but there's no guarantee it will arrive or that you'll know if it doesn't.
keyPoints: Connectionless: No handshaking is done before sending data.
Unreliable: Delivery, ordering, and duplicate protection are not guaranteed.
Lightweight: Has minimal header overhead (only 8 bytes) compared to TCP.
commonMistakes: Assuming UDP is always faster than TCP. While it has less overhead, congestion control in TCP can sometimes make it more efficient on busy networks.
Using UDP for applications that require reliable data transfer, like file downloads or web pages, instead of its intended use for real-time streaming or gaming.
